channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: How to save money on your taxes
item.description: You may qualify for tax credits for caring for a child or dependent or investing in clean energy for your home.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow
item.description: PUNXSUTAWNEY, Pa. (WTAJ) — Each year Punxsutawney Phil predicts whether there will be six more weeks of winter or early spring when he emerges from his home in Gobbler's Knob on Groundhog Day. It's official! Punxsutawney Phil saw his shadow, meaning a prediction of six more weeks of winter.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers. The move is expected to trigger a motion of no confidence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow, handlers say, predicting 6 more weeks of wintry weather
item.description: Punxsutawney Phil’s team of top-hatted associates says he has seen his shadow and is predicting six more weeks of wintry weather.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Michigan priest has license revoked after imitating Musk's gesture at Pro-Life Summit
item.description: The priest, Calvin Robinson, attended the National Pro-Life Summit in Washington, D.C. last month. Toward the end of his remarks at the Jan. 25 event, he mimicked Musk’s salute, which made the rounds online.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: A person in a car was the seventh fatal victim of the fiery crash of an air ambulance onto a busy Philadelphia street, authorities said Saturday, as investigators sifted through burned cars, damaged homes and charred debris for clues to determine why the aircraft plummeted shortly after takeoff.
item.dc:creator: MATT ROURKE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID website goes offline in Trump administration's 2-week-old freeze on foreign aid worldwide
item.description: USAID’s website has dropped offline without explanation in the Trump administration's two-week-old funding freeze of U.S. foreign aid and development funding.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her neck
item.description: The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US military conducts airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has conducted airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia, the first attacks in the African nation during President Donald Trump’s second term. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Saturday that the strikes by U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, AAMER MADHANI and OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Costco, Teamsters reach tentative contract agreement, avoiding a strike
item.description: Costco and the Teamsters union have reached a tentative agreement on a new contract, avoiding a strike, the union said Saturday. Teamsters spokesman Matthew McQuaid confirmed the agreement, which will have to be approved by members. Details of the agreement weren't immediately available.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he ordered precision military strike against senior ISIS planner
item.description: President Trump said he ordered precision air strikes on Saturday morning targeting a "senior ISIS Attack planner" and others the unnamed individual recruited and led in Somalia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia mayor confirms 7 dead, including one on the ground, in medical transport jet crash
item.description: Six people on board a medical transport jet that crashed in Philadelphia were killed. That is according to Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: What to know
item.description: Will spring arrive early? We will find out Sunday whether the groundhog Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow or not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Why Trump's meme coin is a cash grab
item.description: The coin – officially called $Trump – briefly ascended into the top 15 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization and attracted over a half-million buyers.
item.dc:creator: Maximilian Brichta Doctoral Student of Communication, University of Southern California, KRON4

item.title: A deadly flight out of Wichita has one of America's most historic aviation cities reeling
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — When American Eagle Flight 5342 took off from Wichita, the Midwest city with a proud aviation history was glowing from a big moment. It had just hosted the next generation of Olympic hopefuls at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An air ambulance has crashed in Philadelphia. What to know about air medical transport
item.description: A medical transport jet carrying a child patient, her mother and four others slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood and exploded in flames.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina put a third inmate to death in four months Friday as it goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr. was executed at 6:27 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration fires prosecutors involved in Jan. 6 cases and moves toward ousting FBI agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday fired a group of prosecutors involved in the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: A number of U.S. government web pages changed or went dark Friday as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is known about the fatal crash of a plane and Army chopper
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades. The crash around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Soldiers in deadly helicopter crash with DC plane identified
item.description: The U.S. Army has identified two of the three soldiers who were killed Wednesday night when their helicopter crashed into an airplane in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tour guide at former Colorado gold mine fell out of elevator after apparently not latching door
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A tour guide killed at a former Colorado gold mine last year fell out of a crowded elevator taking tourists below ground after apparently not latching its door closed, documents obtained by The Associated Press show.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina's ex-lieutenant governor dismisses lawsuit against CNN, has no plans to run again
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's former Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson on Friday withdrew his defamation lawsuit against CNN that challenged a report about him making explicit posts on a pornography website’s message board over a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother, daughter identified as victims in DC plane-helicopter collision
item.description: More details about the 67 people who died in the helicopter and plane crash outside of Washington D.C. are being shared. Several victims were Virginia residents from Loudon and Fairfax counties, ranging from parents to children.
item.dc:creator: Allison Williams, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Department of Education undoes Biden's Title IX rules on gender
item.description: The Department of Education announced Friday it is scrapping former President Biden's 2024 Title IX changes, getting rid of discrimination protection based on gender identity and sexual orientation and changing how sexual assault cases are handled on campus.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Numerous goods could get more expensive due to Trump tariffs, experts say
item.description: The 25% tax that President Donald Trump plans to slap on imports from Canada and Mexico as soon as Saturday could drive up the price of everything from gasoline and pickup trucks, to Super Bowl party guacamole dip.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN AP Economics Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man rams cars while 'upset' over Nashville traffic: Police
item.description: Police say he was "apparently upset" about the congested downtown traffic after the Nashville Predators game.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: Butler Community College instructor killed in DC plane crash
item.description: The National Association of Biology Teachers has confirmed another victim of the Wichita plane crash in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: PETA issues swap deal: Punxsutawney Phil for a vegan cake
item.description: PETA is urging Punxsutawney Groundhog Club to remove Punxsutawney Phil from his Groundhog Day duties.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: 2 arrested in Texas after kidnapping ‘TikTok prank’ near school
item.description: “The children immediately contacted their parents, who notified the Kyle Police Department,” KPD said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: White House rescinds funding freeze, RFK Jr. faces tough questions
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4
